item.title: Retirement: Support for Python-2.7; 3.8 and PowerShell- 7.1; 7.2 will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description: Starting October 1, 2026, the specified runtime versions Python 2.7, Python 3.8, and PowerShell 7.1 & 7.2. will no longer be supported by Automation. Runbooks using these versions may continue to run but will not receive security updates, bug fixes, or o

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Manage Azure Chaos Studio from the Azure CLI
item.description: You can now create and run Azure Chaos Studio resilience Scenarios directly from the Azure CLI with the new az chaos extension — no more hand-assembling REST calls and JSON files. Get started in one step: az chaos setup stands up a workspace, wires up per

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Open AI GPT-5.6 on Azure Databricks
item.description: Open AI GPT-5.6 is now available on Azure Databricks. You can use GPT 5.6 (purchased on Microsoft Foundry) via Model Serving Endpoint in Azure Databricks. Through integrations across Foundry and Azure Databricks, you can securely build, deploy, and manage

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Exceptions in WAF for Azure Application Gateway and Azure Front Door
item.description: Azure Web Application Firewall (WAF) helps protect your web applications from common threats and attacks. In some cases, WAF may block requests that are safe and expected for your application. Existing exclusions help reduce false positives by omitting sp

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Chaos Studio Workspaces and Scenarios
item.description: Azure Chaos Studio now supports Workspaces and Scenarios, a faster, application-centric way to validate how your workloads hold up during real outages.Point a Workspace at your application's scope—a subscription, resource group, or service group—and Chaos

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Export historical data from Log Analytics workspace with Export jobs
item.description: The Log Analytics Export job enables you to export historical data from a Log Analytics workspace to an Azure Storage account based on a specified query and time range. This allows you to extract only the data you need and move it to external systems for

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Security Perimeter support for Azure Event Hubs
item.description: Azure Event Hubs now supports Network Security Perimeter (NSP), enabling organizations to define a logical network isolation boundary for PaaS resources and control public network access through perimeter-based access rules.With Network Security Perimeter

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Confidential Computing support for Azure Event Hubs Dedicated
item.description: Azure Event Hubs Dedicated now supports Confidential Computing, enabling organizations to protect streaming data while it is being processed in memory. By leveraging hardware-based trusted execution environments (TEEs), customers can help safeguard sensit

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Chile Central
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) is now generally available in Azure Chile Central, expanding regional availability for OpenShift deployments across South America. With the launch of Microsoft’s first Azure region in Chile, customers can now run fully manage

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP
item.description: Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP is now generally available in all regions. You can now use Entra ID identities including guest users via Entra External Identities to securely connect to Azure Blob Storage over SFTP, without pro

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Support 5x churn in Azure Site Recovery
item.description: Azure Site Recovery now supports up to 5x churn (500 MB/s per VM). This major enhancement empowers customers to confidently run high IOPS workloads with Azure Site Recovery, ensuring robust disaster recovery for even the most demanding applications like d

item.title: [Launched] Generally available: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 5 on Azure Databricks
item.description: Azure Databricks now supports Anthropic Claude Sonnet 5 through Azure Databricks AI Model Serving. Claude Sonnet 5 is Anthropic's most agentic Sonnet model to date, delivering near-Opus-level intelligence with Sonnet's cost efficiency and speed. Designed

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: New Powershell module: Az.PostgreSQLFlexibleServer
item.description: We’re excited to introduce the newly renamed Az.PostgreSQLFlexibleServer PowerShell module, delivering a more streamlined experience for you to manage Azure Database for PostgreSQL with PowerShell. Building on the capabilities of the previous Az.PostgreSq

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Document PII playground sample in Microsoft Foundry NextGen
item.description: Document-based PII detection in Azure AI Language now ships an initial playground experience in the Microsoft Foundry NextGen portal. The playground loads a prepared sample document, runs it through the document-based PII feature, and shows the redacted o

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Document PII NextGen Playground in Azure AI Language
item.description: Azure AI Language now offers a refreshed Document PII playground in general availability, giving customers a faster way to evaluate personally identifiable information detection on documents. The playground ships with curated sample inputs and outputs, so

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Instant Access via application consistent restore points
item.description: Azure introduces Instant Access for VM Restore Points, enabling you to restore disks immediately after a restore point is created—without waiting for background data replication.Key benefits:Faster recovery (lower RTO): Restore disks instantly—no wait for

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure Storage Mover now supports migration from Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
item.description: Azure Storage Mover now supports cloud-to-cloud migration of data from Google Cloud Storage (GCS) to Azure Blob Storage, helping organizations simplify multi-cloud consolidation onto Azure.Using the S3-compatible interface, customers can migrate data from

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Client-side data integrity protections in Azure Blob Storage
item.description: Azure Blob Storage has long supported data integrity validation, starting with MD5 as part of the original HTTP standards and adding CRC64-NVME support in 2019. Today, CRC64-NVME is now integrated into the latest Azure Blob SDKs for .NET, C++, and JavaScr

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Toolboxes in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry brings Toolboxes to GA, providing a unified endpoint to invoke the most-used tools in prompt agents and callable outside of prompt agents. Today every agent team hand-assembles its own tool list to solve the same tasks, with no shared ar

item.title: Retirement: Migrate from Azure Blueprints by January 31, 2027
item.description: In September 2023, we announced Azure Blueprints would retire on July 11, 2026. We're extending that timeline to January 31, 2027, with phased retirement beginning July 31, 2026.What's changing?July 31, 2026, you won't be able to create new blueprint defi

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Application Gateway for Containers – Inference gateway
item.description: Application Gateway for Containers is extending its ingress gateway feature set with new AI gateway capabilities. The new inference gateway capability brings the Kubernetes Gateway API Inference Extension to Application Gateway for Containers, enabling lo

item.title: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files migration assistant
item.description: Azure NetApp Files migration assistant (with SnapMirror) provides efficient and cost-effective data migration leveraging ONTAP's built-in replication engine for seamless transition from on-premises or CVO/other cloud providers to Azure NetApp Files (ANF).

item.title: Retirement: Azure VMware Solution AV36 Node Retirement now on September 30, 2027
item.description: Microsoft announced the retirement of the AV36 node type on June 30, 2028, that date has changed to align with VMware’s roadmap. Broadcom will end support for the current AV36 version with vSphere 8 on September 30, 2027, and the AV36 SKU is not compatibl

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Migrate – GitHub Copilot Modernization integration for at scale code assessments
item.description: Azure Migrate now integrates with GitHub Copilot Modernization (public preview) to deliver at scale code insights. This capability brings together Azure Migrate’s portfolio-level discovery and assessment with GitHub Copilot’s context-aware code analysis.

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks native read access to Microsoft OneLake
item.description: Azure Databricks now supports native read access to data stored in Microsoft OneLake through Unity Catalog. This capability is generally available. Customers can query and analyze data directly in OneLake without moving or copying data, enabling faster ac

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks natively storing data in Microsoft OneLake
item.description: Azure Databricks can now natively write managed Delta tables to Microsoft OneLake. This capability enables customers to use OneLake as a unified storage layer for Azure Databricks workloads without managing separate storage accounts. By storing data direc

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: ICMP Support for Azure Standard V2 NAT Gateway
item.description: Azure StandardV2 NAT Gateway now supports outbound ICMP Echo Request and Echo Reply traffic, enabling customers to use tools such as ping to quickly validate outbound connectivity and troubleshoot network issues from workloads behind StandardV2 NAT Gatewa

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: New project templates and template gallery for Azure Functions in VS Code
item.description: The Azure Functions extension for VS Code now provides a redesigned Create New Project experience in public preview, featuring a rich, visual template gallery that replaces the multi-step Quick Pick wizard with a searchable, filterable view of project tem

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Log Analytics Summary Rules experience
item.description: A new Azure portal experience for Summary Rules in Log Analytics is now available. Summary Rules enable aggregation of high-volume log data at a defined cadence and store it in summarized tables for improved query performance, reporting, or enhanced data

item.title: Retirement: Av2-series, F-series, Fs-series, Fsv2-series, G-series, Gs-series, and Lsv2-series Virtual Machines for Azure Batch pools
item.description: Azure Compute will retire the following Virtual Machine series on November 15, 2028, impacting Azure Batch pools that rely on them:Av2-seriesF-series, Fs-series, Fsv2-seriesG-series, Gs-seriesLsv2-seriesAfter this date:New Batch pools using these VM serie

item.title: Retirement: D-series, Ds-series, Dv2-series, Dsv2-series, and Ls-series Virtual Machines for Azure Batch pools
item.description: Azure Compute will retire the following Virtual Machine series on May 1, 2028, impacting Azure Batch pools that rely on them:D-series, Ds-seriesDv2-series, Dsv2-seriesLs-seriesAfter this date:New Batch pools using these VM series cannot be createdExisting

item.title: Retirement: GPv1 and Legacy Blob storage account creation
item.description: As part of our ongoing efforts to streamline the Azure Storage portfolio and deliver enhanced performance, scalability, and cost efficiency, we are announcing the retirement of all General purpose v1 (GPv1) storage accounts. This includes legacy blob stor

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: SQL MCP Server
item.description: SQL MCP Server is now generally available, giving customers a secure, high-performance way to build agentic solutions with controlled access to production data. Built for SQL and compatible with PostgreSQL and Azure Cosmos DB, SQL MCP Server is a dynamic,

item.title: Retirement: Azure Load Balancer Inbound NAT rule version 1 for Azure VMSS (aka Inbound NAT Pools)
item.description: Update to our previous communication: We previously announced the retirement of all Inbound NAT rules version 1 resources, which included both single-VM NAT rules and NAT pools for Virtual Machine Scale Sets. We have narrowed the scope of this retirement

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Entra server principals on Azure SQL Database
item.description: Microsoft Entra server principals (logins) for Azure SQL Database is now generally available. You can now use CREATE LOGIN ... FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER in the virtual master database, bringing parity with SQL logins for your Microsoft Entra identities.With

item.title: Retirement: Azure Synapse Link for Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL
item.description: As of March 31, 2026, new Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL accounts can no longer enable Azure Synapse Link. Existing customers will remain fully supported until March 31, 2029, when Azure Synapse Link will reach end of life.We encourage all customers to begin migra

item.title: Retirement: Azure VPN Client for Linux (Preview) will be retired on August 31, 2026
item.description: The Azure VPN Client for Linux (Preview) has remained in public preview and does not have a path to general availability (GA). As part of our ongoing efforts to align Azure networking services with current security and reliability standards, this preview

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Agent mode for GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
item.description: You can now use Agent mode for GitHub Copilot in SSMS to work with your database: investigating performance problems, tuning queries, reviewing maintenance and configuration, identifying security concerns, troubleshooting errors, and assisting with operat

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL Hub for Azure Developers
item.description: PostgreSQL Hub for Azure Developers brings together key AI and application development resources for PostgreSQL on Azure. It includes curated sample apps and solution accelerators, tutorials, and structured learning pathways to help you build everything f

item.title: [Launched] Update: Minimum billable object size for cooler storage tiers
item.description: We are pausing the introduction of a minimum billable object size for the cool, cold, and archive access tiers. As a result, billing behavior will not change on July 1, 2026, for either new or existing storage accounts. We will provide an update on our r

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure NC RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition v6 Series Virtual Machines
item.description: Azure NCv6-series virtual machines (VMs) are now generally available in the Azure Southeast Asia and West US 2 regions. NCv6 VMs are powered by NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition graphics processing units (GPUs) with each GPU featuring 96 GB of

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Premium SSD v2 disks now support non-zonal Azure Virtual Machines
item.description: Azure Premium SSD v2 disks now support non-zonal, single-instance Azure virtual machines in select Azure regions with Availability Zones (AZs). Customers can now deploy Premium SSD v2 with non-zonal virtual machines without selecting an Availability Zone.

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Foundry agent security capabilities in Microsoft Defender for Cloud are transitioning to a Microsoft Agent 365 license
item.description: Starting July 1, 2026, Microsoft Foundry agent security capabilities previously available through Microsoft Defender for Cloud will transition to a Microsoft Agent 365 license. Agent protection in Defender will be powered by Agent 365 observability logs a

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Site Recovery support for Linux Azure VMs with NVMe disk controllers.
item.description: Azure Site Recovery now supports replication and disaster recovery for Linux Azure Virtual Machines running on NVMe-enabled Generation 2 VM families, such as the Da/Ea/Fa v6-series and Ebsv5/Ebdsv5 in the Azure-to-Azure scenario. This support is limited t

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Maintenance control for Azure Database for PostgreSQL - reschedule, apply on demand, view and download
item.description: Enhanced maintenance control capabilities are now generally available for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. These new capabilities give you greater visibility and flexibility in managing platform maintenance events, helping reduce disruption

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Managed Redis now supports Entra ID based RBAC for data management
item.description: With role-based access control (RBAC) for Azure Managed Redis data access, you can precisely control who can read, write, or administer your Redis data using Microsoft Entra ID—without relying on shared keys. Built on top of Redis Access Control Lists (AC

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Ingestion Volume Change dashboard in Metrics Usage Insights
item.description: Metrics Usage Insights now includes an Ingestion Volume Change dashboard to help you compare ingestion volume over time. Quickly detect spikes or drops in time series counts and event ingestion rates, enabling faster investigation of cost changes and more

item.title: [In development] Private Preview: Guest RDMA on Azure Boost
item.description: Guest RDMA is now in preview on Azure, starting in the UK South region, bringing high-throughput, ultra-low latency networking directly into guest VMs within the same region. By using Azure Boost to enable RDMA in the guest OS, applications can bypass the

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Voice sync for avatar support in Voice Live API in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: In public preview, Microsoft Foundry now supports avatar voice sync with custom voices in the Voice Live API, allowing developers to pair branded or persona-specific text-to-speech voices with real-time avatar experiences. This update helps teams building

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Files assessments are now available worldwide using Azure Migrate
item.description: We’re pleased to announce that Azure Migrate now generally supports discovery and assessment of SMB and NFS file shares hosted on Windows and Linux servers. This gives customers a complete, data-driven view of their file share environment and a clear path

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: AI pipelines in Azure HorizonDB
item.description: AI pipelines for Azure HorizonDB is now available in public preview. AI pipelines let you describe an AI data ingestion workflow, chunking, embedding, extraction, generation, and ranking, declaratively in SQL, and run it as a fault-tolerant pipeline insid

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Ingest OTLP signals into Azure Monitor with the OpenTelemetry Collector
item.description: Azure Monitor's support for native ingestion of OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) signals is now generally available. This enables you to send telemetry data directly from OpenTelemetry-instrumented applications and platforms to Azure Monitor. You can configu

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Tool search in Microsoft Foundry toolboxes
item.description: Microsoft Foundry adds tool search to Foundry toolboxes in public preview, giving developers and admins a fast way to find the right tool inside large, multi-team catalogs. As your toolbox grows, sending every tool definition on every turn gets expensive.

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: APIM Support for Foundry Models in Azure AI Search
item.description: Microsoft Foundry brings Azure API Management support to all Foundry model integrations in Azure AI Search, now in public preview. Enterprise platform engineers and AI solution teams running large-scale RAG pipelines can place Foundry and Azure OpenAI mod

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL as a knowledge source in Foundry IQ
item.description: Microsoft Foundry adds Azure SQL Database as a first-class Knowledge Source in Azure AI Search, available in public preview. Enterprise developers and solution architects building Copilot, RAG, and agentic experiences can now expose authoritative tables a

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Discovery
item.description: Microsoft Discovery is now generally available, providing research and development organizations an enterprise platform for building and governing agentic AI workflows across scientific and engineering disciplines. Also available in preview is the Microso

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Private Connectivity for Azure AI Search and Foundry Knowledge Bases
item.description: Azure AI Search and Foundry Knowledge Bases now support private, end-to-end network connectivity between search resources and Foundry services. Customers can route ingestion, enrichment, retrieval, and agent traffic over Shared Private Link or Network Sec

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Fabric IQ Ontology Knowledge Source in Microsoft Foundry IQ
item.description: Microsoft Foundry IQ now treats a Microsoft Fabric Ontology as a federated knowledge source in public preview, giving agents a way to query the semantic layer customers already curate in Fabric. Foundry IQ translates natural language questions into ontolo

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Foundry IQ
item.description: Microsoft Foundry IQ reaches general availability, giving developers a managed knowledge layer that grounds agents in enterprise data without rebuilding retrieval pipelines for each project. Teams connect SharePoint, OneLake, Azure Blob, and other sources

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Content Understanding chunking and image verbalization in Azure AI Search
item.description: Azure AI Search now supports Content Understanding chunking and image verbalization in public preview, expanding the built-in skill that parses rich documents during indexing. Indexers can split documents into semantically meaningful chunks and generate t

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor Service Level Indicators (SLI)
item.description: Azure Monitor now includes Service Level Indicators (SLIs) and Service Level Objectives (SLOs), giving teams a clearer way to measure how customers actually experience their applications. Instead of relying only on infrastructure signals like CPU or isola

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: MAI-Voice-2 in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry now offers MAI-Voice-2 in public preview, a first-party voice model from the Microsoft AI team. MAI-Voice-2 generates natural speech across more than 10 languages and supports voice cloning from a short reference sample, plus voice promp

item.title: [Launched] General Availability: Backup your ledger files for audit with Azure confidential ledger’s new tool
item.description: Ever wondered how to allow auditing personas (i.e. security analyst in your organization or security assessment contractor) the ability to view your ledger files and establish trust in the cryptographic proofs themselves? With the new localized, web ledg

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Text Analytics for Health NextGen Playground in Azure AI Language
item.description: Azure AI Language brings the next-generation Text Analytics for Health (TA4H) playground to general availability in the Microsoft Foundry portal. Clinical informatics teams and ISVs can paste discharge summaries, doctor's notes, or research abstracts and

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: User feedback logging in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: This feature introduces end user feedback logging semantics that enables developers to capture structured feedback signals from real users interacting with AI agents—such as thumbs up/down, ratings, or custom annotations—and automatically associates that

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Self-serve custom photo avatar creation in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry brings self-serve custom photo avatar creation to GA in the Foundry NextGen fine-tuning portal. Enterprise customers building voice agents with avatars or producing content with avatar and speech often need a branded avatar instead of a

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Belgium Central
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) is now generally available in Azure Belgium Central, expanding regional availability for OpenShift deployments across Europe. With the launch of Microsoft’s first Azure region in Belgium, customers can now run fully managed O

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Agent-to-agent (A2A) support for Prompt agents and Hosted agents in Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service adds agent-to-agent (A2A) communication in public preview for Prompt agents and Hosted agents that use the responses protocol. Agents can call other agents by name through a managed endpoint, with identity, content safety,

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Conversational PII NextGen Playground in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: In public preview, Microsoft Foundry now offers a Conversational PII (ConvPII) NextGen Playground with transcript input and an API Configuration Panel, enabling developers to validate PII detection across multi-speaker dialogue before integrating the API.

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: TextPII NextGen Playground updates in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: In public preview, Microsoft Foundry now offers an updated TextPII NextGen Playground with a refreshed API Configuration Panel, including preview features introduced at Ignite 2025. This update lets developers test predefined PII categories, redaction mod

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Observability developer experience in Azure Developer CLI (azd)
item.description: Use the Azure Developer CLI (azd) CLI evaluation experience to add a measured quality loop to an agent created with Microsoft Foundry. It empowers users on the hosted-agent lifecycle in azd, where you create, provision, deploy, initialize evaluation asset

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Domain filter for specialized model discovery in Foundry model catalog
item.description: Microsoft Foundry model catalog adds a domain filter in public preview, helping developers narrow the catalog of over 1,900 models to the ones trained for a specific industry or use case. Filters cover domains such as robotics, biomedical sciences, materi

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Foundry for Visual Studio Code (June Build 2026 refresh)
item.description: The Microsoft Foundry extension for Visual Studio Code reaches general availability with a Build 2026 refresh that brings the full model catalog, model playground, and Hosted agent deployment into the editor. Developers can sign in with their Azure accoun

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Evaluations with Intelligent Trace Sampling
item.description: Microsoft Foundry observability introduces intelligent trace filtering and sampling for evaluations in public preview. Instead of running evaluations against all production traces, Foundry now select a representative subset of traces using a multi-stage M

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Custom Voice portal experience in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Azure AI Speech moves the Custom Voice authoring experience into the Microsoft Foundry portal in general availability. Approved Custom Voice customers can now upload voice talent recordings and consent statements, run data quality checks, train neural voi

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Rubric evaluator in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry introduces rubric evaluators for measuring agent quality, now in public preview for single-turn and multi-turn agent flows. Rubric evaluators enable developers to define context-specific evaluation criteria using agent prompts, productio

item.title: [In development] Announcing: Confidential Live Migration for Intel TDX confidential VMs in Azure
item.description: Microsoft is announcing Confidential Live Migration in Azure, a new capability designed to improve operational flexibility and availability for Intel® TDX Confidential VMs during platform servicing. Confidential Live Migration helps move a Confidential VM

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Infrastructure Resiliency Manager
item.description: Azure Infrastructure Resiliency Manager is now in public preview, offering a unified, goal-driven experience to design, assess, and improve application resiliency across Azure. It brings together capabilities across Availability Zones, Azure Advisor, Chao

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL - Flexible Server: DuckDB extension
item.description: You can now install the DuckDB extension in Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server. Learn more.

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Advanced full-text search in Azure DocumentDB
item.description: Now you can unify your search operations in a single database. Azure DocumentDB has added fuzzy search, proximity search, more language support, and BM25 ranking to its full-text capabilities, bringing vector and advanced text search operations together.

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Simple log alerts in Azure Monitor
item.description: We are excited to announce the General Availability of Simple log alerts in Azure Monitor, available starting today. This feature is designed to provide a simplified and more intuitive experience for monitoring and alerting, enhancing your ability to dete

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Code-first observability for Foundry Agents in VS Code
item.description: Microsoft Foundry brings code-first observability to agent developers in public preview, delivered as an observe skill inside the Foundry Plugin for VS Code Copilot Chat. Developers run the full evaluation-driven optimization loop from the editor through

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Agent kit for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Your AI coding agents just got smarter about building high-performance database applications. The Azure Cosmos DB Agent Kit is now generally available, bringing proven best practices directly into your development workflow. Whether you're designing data m

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for early-June
item.description: In early June 2026, the following update and enhancement was made to Azure SQL: Now you can use AES 256 symmetric keys to configure transparent data encryption (TDE) with customer-managed keys in Azure SQL Database. Learn more.

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Secure, Modern Access to Azure Files on macOS with MS Entra ID
item.description: Azure Files now supports access from macOS using Entra ID authentication (Public Preview), enabling secure, identity-based access to file shares for cross-platform teams. This expands support for modern workflows, allowing users on macOS to seamlessly acc

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: File share centric management model (Microsoft.FileShares) for Azure Files
item.description: The new file share service management experience for Azure Files is now generally available for NFS 4.1 shares on SSD storage. Powered by the Microsoft.FileShares resource provider, file shares are now top-level Azure resources that you can create, secure

item.title: [In development] Preview: Azure Cobalt 200 Arm-based Dpsv7, Dplsv7, Epsv7, Mpsv4, and Lpsv5-series Virtual Machines
item.description: The general purpose Dpsv7 and Dplsv7, memory-optimized Epsv7, high-memory Mpsv4, and dense storage Lpsv5 VMs are now available in preview. These virtual machines deliver significant enhancements over the previous generation Cobalt 100 VMs, offering improv

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Unified Text Translation API in Azure AI Translator
item.description: Azure AI Translator's unified Text Translation API reaches general availability, giving developers a single endpoint that fronts neural machine translation, large language model translation, small language model translation, Adaptive custom translation, a

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure API Management introduces a Unified Model API for multi-model AI applications.
item.description: As organizations adopt multiple LLM providers, developers often need to handle different API formats, SDKs, and integration patterns for each model vendor. This increases application complexity and makes provider switching, failover, and governance more d

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Management Premium v2 and Standard v2 now support wildcard custom hostnames.
item.description: As API estates grow, managing individual custom domains and certificates for every subdomain can quickly become operationally complex and difficult to scale. Without wildcard support, every new subdomain — whether payments.api.contoso.com, inventory.api.c

item.title: [Launched] Global PTU Reservations Are Now Region-Agnostic
item.description: Global Provisioned Throughput (PTU) reservations are now region-agnostic, enabling a single reservation to cover Global PTU deployments across multiple regions. Why it matters:Optimize savings: One reservation can apply across regions, improving utilizati

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Backup for Cosmos DB
item.description: You can now enable vaulted backups for Azure Cosmos DB accounts, protecting mission-critical data with secure, isolated, and resilient backups. Azure Backup for Cosmos DB, now in preview, supports cyber-resiliency and compliance needs, even in scenarios i

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Policy Coverage for Model Router in Foundry Models
item.description: Azure Policy now provides centralized governance for deployments using the model router. Organizations can define and enforce routing standards across their environment, helping ensure that model selection aligns with security, compliance, and operational

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Voice Live integration with Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service is now generally available for Voice Live integration, allowing developers to connect real-time speech-to-text and text-to-speech to a Foundry Agent without writing their own audio pipeline. Existing agent capabilities — in

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Sphere OS version 26.06 is now available
item.description: Azure Sphere OS version 26.06 is now available in the Retail feed. This release includes updates to Azure Sphere OS only; it does not include an updated SDK. If your devices are connected to the internet, they will receive the updated OS from the cloud.Th

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Automatic OS Image Upgrades for VMSS Flex
item.description: Automatic OS Image Upgrades for Virtual Machine Scale Sets (VMSS) using Flexible Orchestration are now in public preview, enabling consistent, fleet-wide OS updates with reduced manual effort. This capability extends automated image upgrades to VMSS Flex,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: OpenTelemetry Metrics, Visualizations, and Enhanced Monitoring in Azure Monitor for Azure VMs and Arc Servers
item.description: OpenTelemetry metrics and visualizations in Azure Monitor are generally available, delivering an enhanced, unified monitoring experience for Azure Virtual Machines and Arc Servers. This release consolidates key monitoring capabilities into a single experi

item.title: [In preview] Public preview: Azure Linux 4.0 for Azure Virtual Machines and VM Scale Sets
item.description: Azure Linux 4.0, Microsoft's first-party Linux distribution purpose-built for Azure, is now available in public preview for Azure Virtual Machines and VM Scale Sets. It brings a modernized stack — including Linux kernel 6.18 LTS, dnf5 package manager, gli

item.title: [Launched] Azure Container Linux (ACL) now generally available on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description: Azure Container Linux is a container-optimized, immutable operating system for AKS node pools — derived from Flatcar Container Linux and built on Azure Linux RPM packages. It delivers a locked-down, minimal host purpose-built for running containerized wor

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid - MQTT v5 Subscription Identifier
item.description: Azure Event Grid Standard Namespace now supports MQTT v5 subscription identifier capabilities in GA. Now each MQTT subscription can include an identifier that is delivered with incoming messages, enabling applications to route events immediately based on

item.title: [In development] Private Preview: multiparty analytics with Azure Confidential Clean Rooms
item.description: Microsoft is announcing the preview of multiparty analytics with Azure Confidential Clean Rooms, resulting in fully managed service that allows customers and their partners to securely analyze privacy sensitive datasets with Apache Spark based big-data an

item.title: [In development] Private Preview: Storage optimized Lasv5 and Laosv5 Azure VM series
item.description: The Lasv5 and Laosv5 storage optimized VMs, based on the 5th Gen AMD EPYC™ processor (Turin), are in preview. Lasv5-series is designed for storage-intensive workloads requiring high disk capacity, throughput, and I/O, while Laosv5-series is well-suited fo

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Github Copilot modernization
item.description: GitHub Copilot modernization agent is now generally available Coordinated orchestration from estate to code. Scale application assessments and upgrades across your entire app portfolio with GitHub Copilot modernization agent (GA), connecti

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Rolling updates in Flex Consumption
item.description: Rolling updates are now generally available in the Flex Consumption plan, delivering zero-downtime deployments with a simple configuration change.Instead of forcefully restarting all instances during code or configuration updates, the platform gracefully

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Dynamic threshold for Log search alerts
item.description: We’re excited to announce the General Availability of Dynamic threshold for Log search alerts! Using dynamic thresholds means you don't have to know the right threshold for each Log alert rule - Dynamic thresholds calculate the most appropriate thresholds

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks workspace-wide Genie MCP for Microsoft Copilot Studio
item.description: A new workspace-wide Genie MCP endpoint enables any Microsoft Copilot Studio agent to query natural-language questions across an entire Azure Databricks workspace through a single endpoint. The endpoint auto-routes across all connected Genie spaces and Un

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks Lakebase branching with GitHub Copilot agent mode
item.description: Developers in Azure Databricks can now create a copy-on-write branch of their Lakebase production database with a single command, connect GitHub Copilot agent mode to the branch endpoint, and debug AI apps and agents against real production data without e

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Improved PDF batch document translation in Azure AI Translator
item.description: Azure AI Translator batch document translation ships generally available improvements for PDF translation. The service uses Azure AI Document Intelligence to recover structure from both digital-native and scanned PDFs, then translates the recognized text

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Image file translation for batch document translation in Azure AI Translator
item.description: Azure AI Translator batch document translation accepts image files as a generally available input type. Supported formats include `.jpeg`, `.png`, `.bmp`, and `.webp`. The service runs OCR on each image, translates the recognized text into the target lang

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Image translation inside Office documents for batch document translation
item.description: Azure AI Translator batch document translation now translates text inside images embedded in Word `.docx` files as a generally available capability. The service detects image regions, runs OCR, translates the recognized text, and re-renders it back into t

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: OneLake catalog integration for Azure AI Search knowledge sources
item.description: Azure AI Search makes OneLake catalog integration generally available for knowledge sources, so customers can register a OneLake item once and reuse it across multiple knowledge sources and agents. The integration honors existing OneLake item-level permis

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Managed virtual network for evaluations in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry makes managed virtual network support generally available for evaluations, so customers can run cloud evaluation jobs against models and agents that sit behind private endpoints. The Foundry-managed VNET handles outbound connectivity to

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: LLM Speech API in Azure AI Speech
item.description: Azure AI Speech makes the LLM Speech API generally available, delivering state-of-the-art LLM-powered transcription and translation for audio files, with multi-lingual support across 25 languages (90+ locales), improved accuracy, expanded regions, and enh

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Oracle schema conversion to Azure PostgreSQL in Visual Studio Code
item.description: You can now convert Oracle database schema objects to Azure Database for PostgreSQL-compatible schema directly in the PostgreSQL extension for Visual Studio Code. The experience provides a project-based workflow for Oracle to Microsoft for PostgreSQL offe

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Pre-upgrade validation checks for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: You can now proactively validate Major Version Upgrade (MVU) readiness for Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server before initiating the actual upgrade process. New pre-upgrade validation checks allow you to independently run upgrade compatibility v

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Defender security assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: You can now use Microsoft Defender security assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL to continuously evaluate your database security posture and identify potential vulnerabilities and misconfigurations. This feature helps you strengthen your security

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Database PostgreSQL - Flexible Server cross-tenant customer-managed keys
item.description: Azure Database for PostgreSQL - Flexible Server now supports cross-tenant customer-managed keys (CMK), in public preview, allowing you to encrypt your data at rest using an Azure Key Vault key that resides in a separate Microsoft Entra tenant from the dat

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server pg_ivm extension
item.description: Now customers can install the pg_ivm extension in Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server. Learn more.

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Foundry Memory preview refresh
item.description: Microsoft Foundry refreshes the Foundry Memory public preview with a redesigned storage model, new retrieval controls, and tighter integration with the Foundry Agent Service tool surface. Agents using Memory can now write, search, and update user-scoped a

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Purview admin access auditing for sensitivity-labeled content in Azure AI Search
item.description: Azure AI Search adds Microsoft Purview admin access auditing for sensitivity-labeled content in public preview. Admin operations that touch Purview-labeled documents inside Azure AI Search, knowledge retrieval, and Foundry IQ knowledge bases now flow into

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Hosted Agents in Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service reaches preview for Hosted Agents, the container-based runtime that lets developers bring their own agent code in any framework and deploy it to Microsoft-managed infrastructure. Each Hosted Agent runs in a per-session VM-i

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Purview sensitivity labels in Azure AI Search knowledge sources
item.description: Azure AI Search adds Microsoft Purview sensitivity label support to knowledge sources in public preview. Labels applied at the source system flow through ingestion into Azure AI Search and into the knowledge bases that ground Foundry agents and copilots,

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: MAI-Transcribe-1.5 in Microsoft Foundry model catalog
item.description: Microsoft Foundry adds MAI-Transcribe-1.5 to the model catalog in public preview, Microsoft's next-generation speech-to-text model. The release adds improved accuracy with noticeably lower word error rate on long-tail locales than the previous generation,

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: MAI-Image-2.5 in Microsoft Foundry model catalog
item.description: Microsoft Foundry adds MAI-Image-2.5 to the model catalog in public preview, the next generation of Microsoft's in-house image generation model. The release improves photorealism, prompt fidelity, and generation speed compared with MAI-Image-2, and is tun

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Serverless indexers in Azure AI Search
item.description: Azure AI Search now offers a serverless option for native indexers in public preview, removing the requirement to provision and manage indexer compute for ingestion workflows. Indexer execution scales with workload demand, so customers stop paying for idl

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Neural HD voice update (HDv2.5) in Azure AI Speech
item.description: Azure AI Speech is rolling out an updated Neural HD text-to-speech model, moving customers from DragonHDLatest to HDv2.5. The update raises audio quality scores, adds style and paralinguistic tag support for English voices, and reduces word error rate in

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Toolbox connectors and triggers in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry adds connectors and time-based triggers to the Foundry Tools toolbox in public preview, giving enterprise developers and automation engineers a native way to build event-driven AI agents. Foundry agents today are invoked only through dir

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Custom Avatar and Custom Video portal in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Azure AI Speech brings the Custom Avatar and Custom Video authoring experiences into the Microsoft Foundry portal in general availability. Approved customers can upload an actor's video recording, fine-tune a custom video avatar, and generate synthetic ta

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Trace Replay and trace visualizations for Foundry agents
item.description: Microsoft Foundry observability adds Trace Replay and new trace visualizations in public preview, giving agent developers a faster way to debug multi-step runs. The replay view rewinds a captured trace step by step, showing the model, tool, and inter-agen

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: MCP Server Knowledge Source in Microsoft Foundry IQ
item.description: Microsoft Foundry IQ now supports external MCP servers as federated knowledge sources in public preview, expanding retrieval beyond data customers index themselves. Teams can register MCP servers from systems like Salesforce, Atlassian, Confluence, and Se

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Incremental SharePoint permissions sync for Azure AI Search and Foundry IQ
item.description: Azure AI Search and Foundry IQ Knowledge Sources add incremental SharePoint permissions sync in public preview, keeping document-level, site, and library access control lists current as SharePoint permissions change. Enterprises store business-critical co

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Content Understanding NextGen in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry brings Content Understanding into the portal in public preview, ending the context switch to the standalone Content Understanding Studio for everyday work. Developers get Read and Layout playgrounds inside Foundry for OCR text extraction

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Document translation for image files (synchronous, single document)
item.description: Azure AI Translator now supports synchronous single-document image translation at general availability. Customers can submit a standalone image file (JPEG, PNG, and similar formats) through one synchronous API call and receive the translated result immedi

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure AI Translator SDKs for the latest text translation API
item.description: Azure AI Translator publishes generally available SDKs that target the latest text translation REST API across four languages: C#/.NET, Java, JavaScript, and Python. The SDKs expose Translator API v4 SDK to GA across all four supported programming languag

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Adaptive custom translation in the Azure AI Foundry NextGen playground
item.description: Adaptive Custom Translation (AdaptCT) is generally available in the Azure AI Foundry NextGen playground for Azure Translator. AdaptCT enables enterprises to adapt translations to domain‑specific terminology using small datasets—eliminating the need for tr

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Photo Avatar standard and custom in Azure AI Speech
item.description: Photo Avatar in Azure AI Speech is now generally available in standard and custom flavors. Standard Photo Avatar produces a talking head video from a single photo at 512x512 resolution, and custom Photo Avatar lets you fine-tune the look from your own ima

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Purview sensitivity label auditing in Azure AI Search
item.description: Azure AI Search now emits audit events for Microsoft Purview sensitivity labels carried alongside indexed documents. When the SharePoint in Microsoft 365 indexer is configured to preserve and honor sensitivity labels, query-time filtering decisions and la

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: SharePoint in Microsoft 365 indexer supports ASPX pages and Lists
item.description: The Azure AI Search SharePoint in Microsoft 365 indexer now indexes ASPX site content and SharePoint Lists in addition to document libraries. The indexer connects through a Microsoft Entra application registration with application or delegated permissions

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Content Understanding extractionMode for documents
item.description: Content Understanding in Microsoft Foundry now supports an extractionMode setting that lets you choose how documents are analyzed. Pick a layout-aware mode for structured documents where tables, sections, and reading order matter, or a faster text-only mo

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: GenAI prompt skill and chat completion in Azure AI Search knowledge sources
item.description: Azure AI Search makes the GenAI prompt skill generally available, letting indexers call chat completion models during enrichment without custom code. Authors point the skill at a Foundry-hosted model, write a prompt template that references field values,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Managed identity for Foundry billing calls from Azure AI Search
item.description: Azure AI Search now supports managed identity authentication for the billing operations it issues against Microsoft Foundry resources, replacing the prior key-based flow. Customers assign a system-assigned or user-assigned identity to the search service,

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Image serving in Foundry IQ knowledge bases
item.description: Foundry IQ knowledge bases now surface document-extracted images at query time in public preview. When ingestion pulls figures, screenshots, and diagrams out of PDFs, slides, and Office files, Foundry IQ retains each image alongside the text it appeared w

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: OneLake Catalog integration for Foundry IQ Knowledge Sources
item.description: Microsoft Foundry IQ adds public preview support for OneLake Catalog as a knowledge source, so agents can ground responses on lakehouse tables, files, and shortcuts already governed in Microsoft Fabric. Data engineers register the OneLake catalog once and

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Region-agnostic reservations for Global PTU in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry adds region-agnostic reservations for Global Provisioned Throughput Units (PTUs) at general availability, letting customers commit to PTU capacity once and consume it from any supported region. Previously, a reserved PTU commitment was t

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Speech SDK 1.50 for Azure AI Speech
item.description: Speech SDK 1.50 ships as a generally available release across all supported languages: C#, C++, Java, JavaScript, Python, Objective-C, Swift, and Go. The release rolls up the latest speech-to-text and text-to-speech features behind a stable API surface an

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: CLI and SDK support for Deployment Templates
item.description: Deployment templates in Azure Machine Learning add CLI and SDK support in public preview, so platform teams can create, update, delete, and manage templates from infrastructure-as-code pipelines instead of only the portal. The new commands work with az ml

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Deployment Templates support packaging deployment configurations in registries for curated deployment
item.description: Deployment templates in Azure Machine Learning add public preview support for deploying from registries, expanding the set of model registries that customers can use as the source for a templated deployment. Deployment templates package a model, deploymen

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Benchmark evaluations for fine-tuned models in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Choosing the right AI system is rarely as simple as picking the newest or largest model — and agents add their own layer of variability on top. A model that wins on a public leaderboard can still underperform on the reasoning, math, or domain-knowledge qu

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for MySQL now available in new regions and expanded availability zones
item.description: Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server continues to expand its global footprint with new regional availability and enhanced availability zone support. You can now deploy Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server in the Denmark East, Austria East, and Bel

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server self-service quota management experience
item.description: Managing your quota is now faster, simpler, and more transparent. Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server supports self-service quota management through the Azure Quotas blade in the Azure portal. You can view your current quota usage and limits, request

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for June
item.description: In June 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Execute SQL queries, document with Markdown, and mix kernels like Python using SQL Notebooks in the MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code. Learn more.

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension
item.description: The GitHub Copilot integration with Schema Designer is now generally available in the MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code, expanding the visual schema design experience with AI-assisted capabilities. Based on developer feedback and evolving workflows,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description: The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now offers data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot integration as generally available, bringing guided back-end generation into your development workflow. You can generate REST, GraphQL, and Model Context P

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL Database provisioning in the MSSQL extension
item.description: The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now introduces Azure SQL Database provisioning in public preview, starting with the free tier so you can create and connect to a fully managed cloud database directly from your editor at no cost. With a guided wi

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: MCP toolkit for Azure DocumentDB
item.description: Azure DocumentDB now supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP), available in public preview. With the MCP toolkit, you can connect AI agents and LLM-powered applications directly to your DocumentDB database, enabling natural language queries, document ret

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: BM25 full-text search in Azure HorizonDB
item.description: BM25 full-text search is now available in public preview for Azure HorizonDB. Now you can get production-quality keyword search powered by BM25, the same relevance ranking algorithm used by Elasticsearch, Solr, and Azure AI Search, running entirely inside

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Advanced filtering for DiskANN vector search in Azure HorizonDB
item.description: Advanced filtering for DiskANN vector search in Azure HorizonDB is now available in public preview. With this release, you can combine vector similarity search with metadata filters such as category, date range, price, or tenant ID without the performance

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure HorizonDB Agentic Advisor Solution Accelerator
item.description: Agentic Advisor Solution Accelerator for Azure HorizonDB is now generally available. You can use this financial services-focused reference solution to build multi-agent applications in Python using LangGraph and Mem0, with Azure HorizonDB as the data and

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure HorizonDB
item.description: Azure HorizonDB is a PostgreSQL-compatible database service engineered for performance and built for what's next. It delivers future-proof autoscaling storage up to 128 TB, up to three times faster performance than self-managed PostgreSQL, and rapid compu

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure DocumentDB instant free tier clusters
item.description: With near-instant provisioning of free tier clusters, you can get started immediately on Azure DocumentDB. Learn more.

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure DocumentDB Migration Extension in Visual Studio Code
item.description: Announcing the general availability of the Azure DocumentDB Migration Extension for Visual Studio Code. With this release, you can create, run, and manage migration jobs directly from Visual Studio Code to move data from your on-premises or cloud-based Mo

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Change streams (multi-shard) in Azure DocumentDB
item.description: You can build real-time, event-driven applications with change streams in Azure DocumentDB, now in public preview and featuring multi-node support. You can capture live data changes with controlled polling, helping you power instant analytics, automation,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Graceful failover with zero data loss guarantee
item.description:
Azure DocumentDB is excited to announce the general availability of graceful failovers that promote replica clusters while guaranteeing zero data loss.
This feature is an addition to the already existing force promote feature, which immediately promote

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Service-managed failover in Azure DocumentDB
item.description: Azure DocumentDB is excited to announce the general availability of service-managed failover for multi-region clusters. In the event of a regional outage, Azure DocumentDB will automatically fail over the globally distributed cluster from the primary regi

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Relational Database to Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL Migration Assistant
item.description: You can now more easily migrate from relational databases such as Oracle, SQL Server, PostgreSQL, and MySQL to Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL. The Migration Assistant in the Azure Cosmos DB extension for Visual Studio Code guides you through a structured workf

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Cosmos DB distributed transactions
item.description: Announcing the public preview of distributed transactions in Azure Cosmos DB. You can now perform atomic operations across items and partitions, with changes committing together or rolling back automatically on failure. Distributed transactions eliminate

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Per-partition automatic failover for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Per-partition automatic failover (PPAF) is now generally available in Azure Cosmos DB, bringing faster recovery and higher availability to your mission-critical applications. With PPAF, you no longer need to fail over an entire account during a regional d

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: MCP Toolkit for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: You can use the MCP Toolkit for Azure Cosmos DB, now in general availability, to connect your AI agents and copilots to operational data with enterprise-ready reliability. This release gives you a standardized way to expose Cosmos DB capabilities through

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Introducing AI Assistant in the Azure Cosmos DB extension for Visual Studio Code
item.description: You can now use AI assistance in the Azure Cosmos DB extension for Visual Studio Code to work with your data faster and with less manual effort. You can ask questions in plain English and get optimized Azure Cosmos DB queries, use chat participant command

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Change partition keys in Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL API
item.description: You can now change the partition key of an Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL container directly from the Azure portal with just a few clicks. The process supports both online and offline modes by copying data from the source container to a new container through the C

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: New Azure Cosmos DB cost estimator
item.description: Plan your Azure Cosmos DB deployment with confidence. The Azure Cosmos DB cost estimator is now in public preview, giving you a fast, accurate way to model throughput, storage, regions, and capacity mode before you provision a single account. Whether you

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Cosmos DB all versions and deletes change feed mode
item.description: The Azure Cosmos DB all versions and deletes change feed mode is now generally available, giving you complete visibility into every change that happens in your containers. Unlike the default latest version mode, which surfaces only the most recent state o

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Logic Apps now enables developers to invoke Microsoft Foundry Agents directly from workflows.
item.description: Organizations building AI agents often face a disconnect between where agents are built and where business processes are automated. Now, with improved integration between Azure AI Foundry and Azure Logic Apps, developers can now more easily operationalize

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Anyscale on Azure
item.description: Now in Public Preview, Anyscale on Azure is a cloud-native managed Ray platform for machine learning engineers and AI developers who need to run Python workloads at massive scale without compromising speed or control. It brings the power of Ray’s distribu

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Knowledge as a Service with Azure Logic Apps.
item.description: Most RAG implementations today are complex. Teams must build ingestion pipelines, manage chunking and embeddings, configure vector stores, wire up retrieval layers, and then operate the entire system in production. With Azure Logic Apps, RAG becomes a na

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Logic Apps Automation SKU for agentic business process automation.
item.description: As organizations move toward agentic automation, developers increasingly need a simpler way to combine workflows, APIs, and AI agents into production-ready business processes. Today, building these systems often requires stitching together multiple Azure

item.title: [Launched] Generally available: Generally available: Managed system node pools in AKS Automatic
item.description: Provisioning and maintaining system node pools in AKS requires ongoing work to handle scaling, patching, and availability. Managed system node pools in AKS Automatic remove that responsibility. The platform provisions and operates the infrastructure for c

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Center now provides a data plane MCP server for enterprise-wide discovery of APIs and AI assets.
item.description: As organizations adopt MCP-based tooling and AI agents at scale, developers need a simpler way to discover and connect to the growing ecosystem of enterprise MCP servers, tools, APIs, and AI assets. With this update, Azure API Center introduces a data pl

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Center now supports agent registration, agent assessment, and Git-based synchronization.
item.description: As organizations increasingly build autonomous AI agents to automate workflows and orchestrate enterprise tasks, developers need a centralized way to discover, govern, and reuse these capabilities across teams. With this update, Azure API Center enables

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager for Arc-enabled clusters
item.description: Managing Kubernetes clusters across hybrid and multi-cloud environments is fragmented by default: different consoles, different tools, different operational models. Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager now supports Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes in GA, so any CNC

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Management Premium v2 now supports multiple custom domains
item.description: Organizations often need to expose APIs, developer portals, and management endpoints under different branded or organizational domain names- — for example, api.contoso.com for external partners, apis.hrportal.contoso.com for an internal HR platform, and d

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure API Management now supports token metrics for all token types
item.description: As organizations adopt advanced AI models, understanding token consumption is becoming more complex. Traditional metrics focused primarily on prompt and completion tokens often fail to capture newer token categories such as cached, reasoning, or thinking

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Management now supports content safety controls for MCP and A2A APIs.
item.description: As organizations deploy more agentic applications and MCP-based tools, enforcing consistent safety and compliance policies across agent interactions becomes increasingly important. Many teams today rely on fragmented or custom implementations for content

item.title: [In preview] Public preview: Connector Namespace
item.description: Integrating apps with SaaS and line-of-business systems today means writing custom API client code, managing credentials, and handling retries, pagination, and webhooks for every service. Connector Namespace is a fully managed service providing pre-built

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Logic Apps MCP Server.
item.description: As organizations adopt AI agents and MCP-based tooling, integration teams often need to build and maintain custom API layers just to make existing workflows callable by agents. This additional plumbing increases development effort, operational complexity,

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Logic Apps Codeful Workflows with Logic Apps Standard SDK.
item.description: Modern integration and automation teams increasingly want the flexibility of writing workflows directly in code while still benefiting from managed orchestration, enterprise connectors, and cloud-scale operational capabilities. Traditional workflow models

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Management now supports AI gateway capabilities for Anthropic and Vertex AI models.
item.description: As organizations integrate external AI providers into production applications, platform teams need consistent ways to secure, govern, and observe model interactions across providers. Without centralized controls and telemetry, managing compliance, trouble

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Management workspaces now support the built-in gateway
item.description: Organizations using workspace-based API management models previously needed a dedicated workspace gateway and Premium tier deployment to take advantage of API Management workspaces increasing both cost and operational complexity. With this update, Azure

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Management adds support for Agent-to-Agent (A2A) APIs
item.description: As organizations build more agentic applications, agent-to-agent communication is becoming a critical part of enterprise AI systems. But in many environments, agent traffic operates outside existing API governance layers — making it difficult to apply con

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Cosmos DB global secondary indexes
item.description: Global secondary indexes (GSIs) for Azure Cosmos DB are now generally available, giving you a powerful way to accelerate query performance without adding complexity to your application code. GSIs are read-only containers that automatically stay in sync wi

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Agentic Retrieval Toolkit for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: We are announcing the public preview of the Agentic Retrieval Toolkit for Azure Cosmos DB. Motivated by GraphRAG and multiple-hop reasoning problems, this new capability helps you build advanced retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) applications that go be

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Semantic reranker in Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: You can now improve the relevance of any Cosmos DB query or search with the public preview of semantic reranker in Azure Cosmos DB. Semantic reranker analyzes the meaning of your queries and your data to reorder results based on contextual relevance rathe

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Agent Memory Toolkit for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: You can now accelerate the development of agentic applications with the new Agent Memory Toolkit for Azure Cosmos DB. This toolkit provides a customizable, code-first solution to help you build scalable, durable, and cost-efficient memory systems for your

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Safe key rotation for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: The safe key rotation feature helps you confidently rotate your Azure Cosmos DB account keys by providing visibility into when each key was last used. Without this insight, you risk rotating a key that may still be actively used by your applications, whic

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Integrated embeddings for Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL
item.description: Building AI applications on Azure Cosmos DB just got simpler. With integrated embeddings, now in public preview, Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL can automatically generate and maintain embeddings for your data, without the need to build a separate pipeline. Now

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: OmniVec, an open-source embedding toolkit for Azure
item.description: You can now automate embedding generation from multiple data sources into Azure Cosmos DB and other Azure destinations. Building AI applications often requires stitching together data extraction, embedding generation, and writing to vector databases into

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Cosmos DB Linux emulator (vNext)
item.description: We are announcing the general availability of the Azure Cosmos DB Linux emulator (vNext), a cross-platform, Docker-based emulator for local development and testing against Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL. The vNext emulator runs as a lightweight Docker containe

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Defender for Cloud support for Azure Container Apps (Serverless Containers Posture)
item.description: Customers can now bring Azure Container Apps environments into Microsoft Defender for Cloud’s Serverless Containers Posture experience, helping security teams extend posture management across more of their container estate from a single workflow. This mak

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Confidential Compute support on Azure Container Apps
item.description: Customers running regulated or sensitive containerized workloads often need stronger protection for data while it is actively being processed, especially in scenarios that require higher isolation and stricter compliance controls. Confidential Compute in

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Monitor HTTP traffic in Azure Container Apps
item.description: In addition to platform-level logs, Azure Container Apps now adds a dedicated Azure Monitor diagnostic setting category, ContainerAppHTTPLogs, that exposes detailed HTTP access logs for incoming traffic and is designed for high-volume request data. With t
